steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the circle facts first: radii are equal, a diameter sets up a right angle, a tangent is perpendicular to the radius, and central/inscribed angles connect to arcs.

steps.title: Apply the degree-to-radian conversion formula
steps.content:
To convert from degrees to radians, multiply the degree measure by $\frac{\pi}{180}$. For a 540-degree angle:
$$540^{\circ} \times \frac{\pi}{180} = \frac{540\pi}{180}$$

steps.title: Simplify to find a
steps.content:
Simplify $\frac{540\pi}{180}$ by dividing both the numerator and denominator by 180:
$$\frac{540\pi}{180} = \frac{540}{180} \times \pi = 3\pi$$
Since the angle equals $3\pi$ radians, the constant $a = 3$.
